name: Helix
description: Figure AI's proprietary vision-language-action model powering autonomous humanoid behavior in factory settings. Helix integrates multimodal perception with real-time motor control, enabling Figure robots to perform complex manipulation tasks with minimal human intervention.
keyDifferentiator: End-to-end VLA trained on real-world BMW manufacturing data at production scale
fundingContext: $2.6B total funding at $6.7B valuation (Series B, Feb 2025)

milestones.event: Initial Helix model demonstrated on Figure 01
Figure 02 deployed at BMW Spartanburg with Helix
Series B at $6.7B valuation
Figure 03 launch with Helix v2
BMW scales to 100+ unit deployment

name: pi0
description: Physical Intelligence's foundation model for general-purpose robot control. pi0 achieves zero-shot generalization across diverse robot embodiments and tasks. pi0.5 (April 2025) extended capabilities to long-horizon multi-step tasks with improved spatial reasoning.
keyDifferentiator: Zero-shot cross-embodiment generalization without task-specific fine-tuning
fundingContext: $600M Series A at $5.6B valuation (Nov 2024), backed by Bezos, OpenAI, Thiel

milestones.event: pi0 model announced with cross-embodiment demo
$600M Series A at $5.6B valuation
pi0 open-sourced under Apache 2.0
pi0.5 released with long-horizon task planning
Production deployments begin with logistics partners

name: Skild Brain
description: Skild AI's general-purpose robotics foundation model designed to control any robot form factor. The model is omni-bodied, meaning a single neural network can operate humanoids, quadrupeds, drones, and industrial arms without architecture changes.
keyDifferentiator: Single foundation model controls any robot morphology (omni-bodied)
fundingContext: $600M at $14B valuation (Jan 2026), largest robotics AI round

milestones.event: $300M Series A at $1.5B valuation
Skild Brain demo across 4 robot form factors
Partnerships with 3 major humanoid OEMs
$300M round at $14B valuation

name: GR00T N1
description: NVIDIA's dual-system humanoid foundation model combining System 2 slow-thinking VLM for task planning with System 1 fast diffusion policy for real-time motor control. GR00T N1 runs on Jetson Thor and has become the de facto standard for Western humanoid OEMs.
keyDifferentiator: Dual-system architecture (slow planning + fast execution) on purpose-built Jetson Thor hardware
fundingContext: NVIDIA internal project; Jetson Thor hardware revenue estimated at $2B+ by 2027

milestones.event: GR00T project announced at GTC 2024
Jetson Thor SoC unveiled with 2070 TFLOPS
GR00T N1 model released at GTC 2025
Adopted by 7+ humanoid robot manufacturers
GR00T N1 open-sourced for research and commercial use

name: Gemini Robotics
description: Google DeepMind's multi-model robotics system combining RT-2 for vision-language-action reasoning, AutoRT for autonomous data collection, and SARA-RT for efficient transformer inference. Leverages the full Gemini model family for high-level task understanding.
keyDifferentiator: Multi-model pipeline leveraging Gemini LLMs with specialized robotics modules (RT-2, AutoRT, SARA-RT)
fundingContext: Google DeepMind internal; Boston Dynamics partnership via Alphabet ownership

milestones.event: RT-2 model published with web-scale pretraining
AutoRT autonomous data collection fleet deployed
SARA-RT efficient inference announced
Gemini Robotics ER announced with Boston Dynamics integration
Atlas electric running Gemini-powered manipulation tasks

name: Carbon
description: Sanctuary AI's cognitive architecture combining symbolic reasoning, large language models, deep learning, and physics simulation. Carbon emphasizes explainable AI, allowing operators to understand why the robot makes specific decisions. New tasks can be learned in under 24 hours.
keyDifferentiator: Explainable cognitive architecture that learns new tasks in under 24 hours
fundingContext: $370M+ total funding; Canadian Tire production deployment contract

milestones.event: Carbon AI system first demonstrated on Phoenix
Canadian Tire pilot deployment begins
Task learning time reduced to under 24 hours
Canadian Tire scales to commercial deployment
Carbon v3 with improved explainability and multi-task chaining

name: VLT
trainingDataScale: EV autonomous driving data + humanoid teleoperation
description: Xpeng Robotics' vision-language-action model adapted from their autonomous driving technology. VLT runs on custom Turing AI chips delivering 2250 TOPS, powering the 82-DOF Iron humanoid robot with perception systems derived from Xpeng's EV self-driving stack.
keyDifferentiator: Autonomous driving AI transfer to humanoid control with 2250 TOPS custom Turing chips
fundingContext: Xpeng Inc. internal division; Xpeng publicly traded (NYSE: XPEV, ~$15B market cap)

milestones.event: Iron robot unveiled at Xpeng AI Day with 82 DOF
Turing AI chip announced with 2250 TOPS
VLT model demonstrated with EV-to-humanoid transfer learning
Pre-production Iron units begin factory trials

name: Tesla FSD-Bot
trainingDataScale: Tesla factory + FSD driving data repurposed
description: Tesla's neural network for Optimus humanoid robots, derived from the Full Self-Driving architecture. Trained on Dojo supercomputer clusters, the system repurposes Tesla's massive autonomous driving dataset and factory teleoperation data for humanoid control. Over 8000 Optimus units deployed internally.
keyDifferentiator: FSD autonomous driving neural net adapted for humanoid robots, trained on Dojo supercomputer
fundingContext: Tesla internal (TSLA ~$800B market cap); Musk projects Optimus worth more than car business

milestones.event: Optimus Gen 2 demonstrated with improved hands
Optimus begins internal deployment at Fremont factory
1000+ Optimus units deployed internally
Optimus Gen 3 revealed at Shareholder Day
8000+ Optimus units operating across Tesla factories

name: DYNA-1
description: Dyna Robotics' foundation model achieving 99%+ task success rate in 24/7 continuous commercial operation. DYNA-1 powers a Robot-as-a-Service (RaaS) business model, with the model continuously improving from deployment telemetry data across customer sites.
keyDifferentiator: 99%+ success rate in 24/7 continuous commercial operation with RaaS delivery model
fundingContext: $600M+ total funding; RaaS revenue model with recurring contracts

milestones.event: DYNA-1 model achieves 99% task success in lab
First commercial RaaS deployment
24/7 continuous operation milestone reached
$400M funding round
Multiple enterprise customers on RaaS contracts

name: OpenVLA
description: Open-source 7B parameter vision-language-action model trained on 970K real robot episodes from the Open X-Embodiment dataset. OpenVLA outperforms Google's RT-2-X by 16.5% on generalization benchmarks while being fully open and reproducible.
keyDifferentiator: Fully open-source 7B VLA outperforming RT-2-X by 16.5% on generalization benchmarks
fundingContext: Academic consortium funded by NSF, Toyota Research Institute, and Google DeepMind grants

milestones.event: OpenVLA paper published with 7B model
Model weights and code released under MIT license
Community fine-tuning ecosystem emerges
OpenVLA v2 with improved sim-to-real transfer
Adopted by 20+ research labs worldwide

name: LeRobot
description: Hugging Face's open-source framework for real-world robotics, providing a hardware-agnostic platform that aggregates multiple policy architectures including pi0, OpenVLA, ACT, Diffusion Policy, and TDMPC. LeRobot includes native support for Unitree G1 and simplifies data collection, training, and deployment.
keyDifferentiator: Hardware-agnostic open framework aggregating pi0, OpenVLA, and other policies with plug-and-play deployment
fundingContext: Hugging Face internal ($4.5B valuation); large open-source community contribution

milestones.event: LeRobot v0.1 released with ACT and Diffusion Policy support
Unitree G1 native integration added
pi0 and OpenVLA policies integrated
LeRobot v1.0 with production deployment tooling
1000+ community-contributed datasets on Hugging Face Hub

name: RoboForce FM
description: RoboForce's foundation model built around a data flywheel architecture where each deployment generates training data to improve the next model iteration. NVIDIA partnership provides compute infrastructure and Isaac Sim integration for synthetic data generation.
keyDifferentiator: Data flywheel architecture with continuous model improvement from deployment telemetry
fundingContext: $67M raised; NVIDIA partnership for compute and simulation

milestones.event: $42M Series A with NVIDIA participation
Data flywheel architecture validated in pilot deployments
$25M extension round
Pre-production units begin enterprise trials

name: Mind Cognitive
description: Mind Robotics' software-first cognitive AI system designed for manufacturing environments. The platform prioritizes software intelligence over hardware, using data from Rivian factory operations to train models capable of complex assembly and logistics tasks.
keyDifferentiator: Software-first philosophy prioritizing AI capability over hardware, trained on Rivian factory data
fundingContext: $615M raised; Rivian strategic partnership for factory deployment

milestones.event: Mind Robotics emerges from stealth with $115M seed
Rivian factory partnership announced
$500M Series A
First prototype units enter Rivian factory trials

name: Atlas-Gemini
description: The integration of Google DeepMind's Gemini models with Boston Dynamics' Atlas electric humanoid robot. This hybrid system combines Atlas's world-class hardware control with Gemini's multimodal reasoning for complex manipulation and navigation tasks in industrial settings.
keyDifferentiator: Best-in-class hardware (Atlas electric) paired with frontier AI (Gemini) under Alphabet umbrella
fundingContext: Alphabet internal; Hyundai Motor Group targets 30K Atlas units/year by 2028

milestones.event: Atlas electric platform unveiled, replacing hydraulic version
DeepMind integration begins with RT-2 experiments
Gemini Robotics ER announced for Atlas
Atlas-Gemini demonstrated at Hyundai factory
Hyundai announces 30K units/year target by 2028
